conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point for PBO block
conditions.detector: Density detection system DDS 70 combined with SICON LCD 201 RI detector
conditions.evidence_text: Critical conditions for PBO were realized in 88 wt% THF–water... In the semipreparative LCCC system... Spherisorb ODS2 (250 mm × 10 mm, 5 μm, 80 Å)... temperature (25.0°C for LCCC)... flow rate of 2.0 mL min−1.
conditions.notes: Used as first dimension for separation of block copolymer from homopolymer. Although semi-preparative column dimensions are used, the text explicitly identifies this as LCCC analysis to separate components based on critical conditions.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point for PHO block
conditions.detector: Density detection system DDS 70 combined with SICON LCD 201 RI detector
conditions.evidence_text: Critical conditions for PHO in 92 wt% THF–water... In the semipreparative LCCC system... Spherisorb ODS2 (250 mm × 10 mm, 5 μm, 80 Å)... temperature (25.0°C for LCCC)... flow rate of 2.0 mL min−1.
conditions.notes: Used as first dimension for separation of block copolymer from homopolymer.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point for PEO block
conditions.detector: RI 2414 or SEDEX 45 ELSD coupled with DDS 70
conditions.evidence_text: In the analytical LCCC system (second dimension)... Critical conditions for PEO exist at 75.7 wt% acetone–water... Normal phase column used for LCCC in second dimension was Nucleosil 100-5 OH 5 μm, silica-based diol phase; 4.6 mm × 250 mm; particle diameter: 5 μm; pore diameter: 100 Å... flow rate of 0.5 mL min−1... temperature (25.0°C for LCCC).
conditions.notes: Used as second dimension to separate block copolymer from unreacted MeO-PEO initiator.
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, A-8010 Graz, Austria
